div1.entryFree:
Property, vb. 1) to make a property or tool of: they have here --ed me, keep me in darkness, Tw. IV, 2, 99 (they have done with me as with a thing having no will of its own). I am too high-born to be --ed, to be a secondary at control, John V, 2, 79. his large fortune . . . subdues and --es to his love and tendance all sorts of hearts, Tim. I, 1, 57.
2) partic., or rather adj., --ed, == endowed with qualities: his voice was --ed as all the tuned spheres, Ant. V, 2, 83.*
